module_mood_contract: Unsettled competence — 'I wonder if this is right?' Multi-agent works; the output sits at an uneasy distance from trust. Doubt is planted, deliberately unresolved. Module 5 is the rescue.
trace_note: Phases 0-4 carried from prior trace (phase content unchanged). Phase 5 regenerated: Debrief prompt hardened 2026-05-02 (audit-harshly + two-seams-with-evidence requirement + body callout added). New content_sha: 7a32271df690191e156e2d045bebffcf4f515cf61b3033db08fa7f5f162296dd.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'When to split an agent (and how)' and 'Debugging stuck agents' are placed AFTER the three exercises. 'Demo: Agent that takes action' is an opener showing a ceiling — correctly placed before exercises. Placement honours opener/closer distinction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pre-exercise content: one boundary-shift paragraph + one demo lecture link. No framework mapping, no practitioner-term naming, no tradeoff explanation before exercises. 'When to split' stays as closer. Dosage is minimal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 (Three retrievers): three agent sessions run concurrently while student monitors. Phase 2 (Three minds): subagents run in parallel inside one session. Both are walk-away-adjacent — student sets up and observes, not reads-and-pastes every reply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: 50% magic rule (M3–M8).
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 is one of the three designated magic beats (content-strategy: M3 multi-session/multi-agent coordination). Four windows churning simultaneously is the magic moment. Magic is understandable: student names seams in the Debrief.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Homework optional memory pass: 'Skip if memory's still serving you.' No yoga-instructor pacing or forced pauses in module spine. 'Build' invitation in Next is open, not commanded.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt (fenced): 'Audit harshly. Diplomatic summaries are a failing grade here.' / 'Name at least TWO handoff seams where the rules wobbled, with specific evidence: name the file, the pass, and what was lost. A seam without evidence doesn't count.' Both forcing functions are inside the fenced block. The body callout after the prompt is post-run optional reflection — not a forcing function in prose, and optional by carve-out (compounded 2026-05-02). Homework prompt (fenced): 'Name at least one drop candidate; an all-green health check means you didn't look hard enough.' In the prompt. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Measure silent failure, not just friction.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Content-strategy: 'The output is meant to feel 7/10, not 10/10.' Module body: 'The unease you feel about the synthesized briefing is not something to close today. It stays.' Frictionless = wrong edit is baked into the design. No silent-failure risk — competent execution still lands the unease.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Hard-grep of student-facing body (lines 1–81) for banned phrases — 'share with your neighbor', 'sitting next to you', 'tell the room', 'discuss with', 'compare with', 'take 90 seconds', 'the person next to you': none found. The 'room' mention on line 113 is in the maintainer block (below <!-- maintainer --> at line 82). Body callout after Debrief prompt is about the WORK (why the forcing function works) not cohort management. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-report and self-audit charity — verify at artifact, not summary.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt: 'Name at least TWO handoff seams with specific evidence: name the file, the pass, and what was lost. A seam without evidence doesn't count.' Homework prompt: 'Name at least one drop candidate; an all-green health check means you didn't look hard enough.' Both force artifact-level evidence. Body callout: 'Diplomatic is the failure mode at a handoff seam.' PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both fenced prompt blocks (Debrief + homework) contain no [BRACKET] placeholders. All path references are deterministic (agents/, sources/, module-3/, memory/, CLAUDE.md).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt: preceded by 'Five minutes. Claude reviews the session and sharpens the rules...' (semantic lead-in in section header context). Homework prompt: 'run this between modules to let the new evidence pressure-test the memory's shape' as lead-in sentence before the block. Both satisfy the semantic lead-in requirement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompt blocks use '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' label with blank line before fenced block. No blockquote wrapping, no '*(end of prompt)*' closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief fenced block and homework fenced block both use plain bullets/text. No **bold** or *italic* markdown inside either fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Agents 101-specific).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt references 'agents/, sources/, module-3/' and 'CLAUDE.md at the root' — root-level intent is clear from the CLAUDE.md label. Homework references 'memory/ and sources/'. All are explicit subpath references. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 mood contract: 'Unsettled competence — I wonder if this is right?' Honoured explicitly: 'The unease you feel about the synthesized briefing is not something to close today. It stays. Hold it as a question, not a fix.' Key Concepts name seams and beige-averaging as where failure lives. Big Idea names the mechanism without resolving quality.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood not punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closing unease is 'I wonder if this is right?' — not-knowing, not having-done-wrong. Debrief audits the SYSTEM (agents, seams, handoff rules), never the student's choices. M4 bridge: 'What's the worst thing it could do with that?' — system framing. No shame framing found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Hire three agents to search. Three more to decide. The filesystem is the meeting room.' — positive discipline (orchestration, coordination). 'Seams are where it fails' appears in Key Concepts as the negative case the discipline addresses, not the title framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Content-strategy M3: two shapes (separate sessions + subagents), Rumelt synthesizer spine, three stances (planner/experimenter/reframer), output 7/10 by design, unease as handoff to M5. Module matches all: two shapes in Key Concepts, Rumelt named as synthesizer spine, three stances named, mood unresolved at close. No front-running M4/M5 teaching moments.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Agents 101 themes: multi-agent orchestration, seam discipline, framework-guided judgment, compounding. Key Concepts: 'Two multi-agent shapes' (orchestration), 'Splitting earns its keep when agents genuinely can't be one' (discipline), 'Seams are where it fails' (seam discipline), 'Three stances beat one summarizer' (orchestration), 'Frameworks are the synthesizer's spine' (framework-guided judgment). At least 3 theme instances present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not a standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Bullets are tactical in module's own voice. Natural bolded phrasing in bullets ('Splitting earns its keep when...', 'Seams are where it fails.', 'Frameworks are the synthesizer's spine.') function as implicit breadcrumbs. Optional formal '**Remember: ...**' breadcrumbs absent but rule marks these as 'on 1-3 bullets where a theme naturally fits' — existing bold phrases serve the same function. TODO: consider adding 1 formal breadcrumb to the Seams bullet.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one **Remember:** breadcrumb to the Seams bullet, e.g. 'Remember: the seam is where the lesson lives.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — all phase scores 8+.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Sim trace phase scores: [8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 9]. Close score: 8. Phase 5 (Debrief) raised from 8 to 9 after hardening: forced specificity (two seams with evidence) adds trust in the audit without resolving the strategic doubt. All beats ≥ 8. No beats below 7.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The hardened Debrief prompt and body callout explain audit mechanics (seam-level), not strategic correctness (output-level). The closing paragraph: 'The unease you feel about the synthesized briefing is not something to close today. It stays.' M3's strategic uncertainty is explicitly preserved. The harsh audit surfaces more to be uncertain about, not less — mood-consistent with unsettled competence. M5 owns the rescue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — seams as site of failure.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment per content-strategy: 'Seams are where it fails. Synthesizer left un-prompted averages three takes into beige.' Key Concepts: 'Seams are where it fails. Conflicts get dropped, dialects clash, the synthesizer averages everything into beige. Where the pieces meet is where the lesson lives.' Debrief prompt structurally enforces it: 'Name at least TWO handoff seams where the rules wobbled, with specific evidence.' Teaching moment is named AND structurally forced. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Hire three agents to search. Three more to decide. The filesystem is the meeting room.' Exercise structure: Three retrievers (search) + Three minds subagents + synthesizer (decide). Filesystem is the literal shared medium. Counts map: 3 retrievers = 'three to search'; 3 stances + 1 synthesizer = 'three more to decide' (close enough — the strategy doc says '3+1' as the shape). Big Idea is accurate to the exercise design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — M3 hands off to M4.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge (line 64): 'You just built something that works across three systems. Which means it just gained access to three systems. What's the worst thing it could do with that?' Content-strategy M4 mood: 'I'd like to be safe and I genuinely don't know how to know.' The bridge provokes the M4 question without answering it. PASS.

todos: check_strategy_tie_in rule 6: Key Concepts lacks a formal **Remember:** breadcrumb on any bullet. The natural bold phrasing in bullets serves the intent but adding one explicit breadcrumb (e.g., on the Seams bullet: 'Remember: the seam is where the lesson lives.') would strengthen theme anchoring.

Vocabulary-before-context: 'subagents' appears in What You'll Learn LOs (line 13) before the lecture that defines it. Non-blocking (Teacher Claude bridges in self-study, trainer in cohort), but a polish candidate for when the LO wording next gets touched.
